insight: Centrifuge (event unit) was considered the worst unit ever designed, with masn6811 claiming it caused a p1 autowin in 90-93% of sets at cost 1.1, making it a higher-priority hotfix than 11GGBB Magna.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811's mid-event unit review of balance changes, rating each change as supported or unsupported.

insight: Chrono Filter's new click-to-activate version (vs. automatic start-of-turn activation) was deemed to make no difference in 90-93% of games, limiting its practical impact despite being theoretically interesting.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 reviewing mid-event balance changes, evaluating which ones meaningfully affected gameplay.

insight: The Mega Drone change (splitting its gold generation into passive +1 per turn and clickable +3 additional) was seen as making it a more boring unit, since the click would almost never be used when Wall or better absorbers are available.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 critiquing the event patch changes, arguing Mega Drone's new design reduces decision points without adding meaningful gameplay.

insight: Aegis was already stronger than Innervate before its heal-1hp-per-turn buff; the buff was considered unnecessary added complexity, and if the goal was to make Aegis stronger relative to Polywall, a cost reduction to 7GG would have been a cleaner approach.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 evaluating the Aegis buff in the mid-event patch, with velizar_ agreeing the heal mechanic was not the right way to buff it.

insight: Gauss Charge (event version that comes with a second copy at buildtime 3) was considered potentially too pushed; masn6811 originally suggested buildtime 5 as balanced, and velizar_ proposed buildtime 4 as a middle ground, noting the unit suffers in sets without a green sink to absorb floated green.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion following masn6811's replay spotlight showing a Gauss Charge rush win, analyzing the unit's strength and the problem of green float when supply runs out.

insight: Gauss Charge rush suffers from floating large amounts of green once its supply is exhausted if no green sink (e.g., Cluster Bolt, Cryo Ray, clickable green-to-gold drones) is present in the set.
author: velizar_
context: velizar_ analyzing masn6811's Gauss Charge rush replay spotlight, identifying the structural weakness of the strategy in sets without green sinks.

insight: Defense Grid does not necessarily need to absorb damage to be valuable; its presence alone can force the opponent to spend resources (e.g., 12RR on 4 threat) in response to the threat it implies.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 posting a replay spotlight demonstrating Defense Grid winning without absorbing, by shaping opponent's spending decisions.

insight: Buying Aurides allows transitioning to higher economy while spending resources efficiently every turn, a role that purchasing Drones cannot fill in the same way; no other unit provides the same sustained economic momentum.
author: velizar_
context: velizar_ commenting on masn6811's Endotherm Kit / Auride transition replay, contrasting Auride's sustained econ role against drone-buying.

insight: Mass-buying event Fission Turrets from the start is a losing strategy unless economies are very low or there is green synergy (e.g., Cluster Bolt, Cryo Ray); the unit is not inherently weak but opening with many is suboptimal.
author: silentslayers
context: silentslayers critiquing a replay where an opponent opened with mass Fission Turrets, explaining when the unit is and isn't viable.

insight: Wincer combined with Magna Kronus can systematically deny absorb from large absorbers in a set, resulting in a game state where neither player benefits from absorb at all.
author: velizar_
context: velizar_ reporting on a game outcome where the Wincer + Magna combination neutralized all absorb options for both players.

insight: Wincer remains very strong even after its +R cost nerf, to the point where it is often correct to prioritize buying it over other options, and skipping it (e.g., in favor of Apollo) is typically a mistake.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 critiquing an opponent's build that skipped Wincer in favor of Apollo, arguing Wincer's post-nerf power level still warrants high priority.

insight: 8BX Lancetooth is stronger than average for its color cost and can sometimes cause a player to stop econing a turn early; masn6811 argues that if 8BX Lancetooth is already strong, then the previous 6BXX version was significantly overpowered.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 entering a discussion where velizar_ and silentslayers were debating whether 8BX Lancetooth is too weak compared to other blue attackers.

insight: Player 2 has a structural advantage in fast red sets because P2 acquires Animus first, which is favored given how red units work (aggressive with low defensive potential); in green sets P2's natural Conduit is significantly more efficient than P1's slow Conduit, giving P2 a tech timing advantage.
author: xyotsuba
context: xyotsuba explaining the structural sources of P2's overall higher win rate, specifically identifying fast red and green sets as P2-favored archetypes.

insight: Adding a second pivot (e.g., an Energy Pivot alongside Gaussite Pivot) to the base set creates approximately twice the cognitive load of a single pivot, and would therefore need to provide nearly twice the balance benefit to be worthwhile.
author: velizar_
context: velizar_ arguing against adding a second pivot unit to the base set alongside Gaussite Pivot, discussing the tradeoff between balance improvement and added complexity.

insight: Even buffed Apollo cannot beat Vivid Drone strategies; masn6811 also reports losing a P1 Synth Apollo + Aegis game to Flame Animus + Bull, suggesting Apollo remains too weak even with buffs.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 posting replay highlights as evidence that Apollo's buffed version still loses to strong alternative strategies.

insight: When a Sentinel (event unit referenced as 'sent') can be clicked into a Wall, it is correct to do so when the resulting Engineer is worth more than the Stamina lost; masn6811 frames this as comparing a plain Sentinel vs. a 2-Stamina Sentinel plus an Engineer for the same cost.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 doing inflation calculations after a Hellhound/Rager game and discovering a misplay where clicking Sentinel into Wall was correct.

insight: Prompt units constructed during a turn (e.g., via a Start-of-Turn construct ability) cannot be clicked or consumed on the same turn they are created; this is consistent with the restriction that also prevents prompt Rhino (from Mobile Animus) from being clickable on creation.
author: consensus
context: Discussion of the Yggdraseed custom unit design, where masn6811 and randomflyingtaco clarified that prompt does not grant same-turn usability to constructed units.

insight: Galvani Drone is so broadly synergistic that it is considered broken with almost any unit that generates extra resources; masn6811 specifically flagged it as a problem with Automaid producing XE, and silentslayers noted it as a persistent interaction issue.
author: consensus
context: masn6811 asking Elyot about making Automaid produce XE, noting it would be broken with Galvani but that Automaid is already broken with Galvani; shadourow and velizar_ agreeing Galvani breaks most things.
